subject: geography
text: Industries are broadly classified into

options.B: mining, furniture making and cocoa processing

explanation: Industries are broadly classified into primary, secondary and tertiary. The primary sector involves the extraction of raw materials, the secondary sector involves the manufacturing of goods, and the tertiary sector involves the provision of services.

explanation: The Greek system of climate classification divides the world into; torrid, temperate and frigid zones

subject: geography
text: Which of the following is not a problem facing transportation development in Tropical Africa?
explanation: All the options listed are problems facing transportation development in Tropical Africa except for 'Climatic variation'. While climatic variation can affect transportation, it is not a major problem facing its development in Tropical Africa.

subject: geography
text: Town C is located on latitude 15oE while Town D is located on latitude 60oE. If the local time in Town C is 7.00am., what will be the local time in Town D?
explanation: Town C(15oE) Town D(60oE) 7:00am ---add---> x Note: 15oE comes first 60oE longitude difference (60-15) same longitude youn substract 45o convet 45o to time 15o → 1hr 45o → 3hrs ∴ Time at town D = + + 3 = 10:00am

subject: geography
text: A smaller range of temperature is recorded along the coast than the continental interior on the same latitudes because of the
explanation: The moderating influence of the sea is the reason why a smaller range of temperature is recorded along the coast than the continental interior on the same latitudes. This is because water has a higher specific heat capacity than land, meaning it can absorb and release heat more slowly than land. This results in cooler summers and warmer winters along the coast, leading to a smaller temperature range.

subject: geography
text: Which of the following represents the correct sequence in which denudation occurs?
explanation: Weathering ( breakdown of rocks into fragments ) erosion( transportation of the fragment away from the original location ) deposition(settling of fragment in a new location )

subject: geography
text: Rocks formed through cementation of accumulated materials under water is ?
explanation: sedimentary rock is a type of rock that forms from the accumulation and compression of sediment such as mineral particles ,organic matter and rock fragments examples are shale, siltstone, sandstone, and breccia

subject: geography
text: An instruction of thick acid lava, which forces overlaying sedimentary rock layers to bulge into a dome, may cool and solidify to form a
explanation: A laccolith is a type of igneous intrusion that forms when thick, acidic lava pushes up overlaying sedimentary rock layers, causing them to bulge into a dome shape. As the lava cools and solidifies, it forms a distinctive body of rock

subject: geography
text: The Greek system of classifying climate is based on
explanation: The Greek system of weather classification is based on temperature and rainfall

subject: geography
text: What will be the time at Greenwich meridian when it is 12 noon at longitude 60\(^0\)E
explanation: The difference in longitude between the Greenwich meridian (long,0\(^0\) ) and longitude 60\(^0\)E =60\(^0\) For a longitude difference of 15\(^0\) , there will be a time difference of 1 hr Thus, for 60\(^0\) , the time difference will be \(\frac{1hr}{15º} \times 60º \) =4hrs since we have traveled west of 60\(^0\)E to reach 0\(^0\) ( Greenwich meridians) We subtract time Thus .if it is 12 noon at long 60\(^0\)E the time at Greenwich meridian =14noon -4hrs =8.00 am

subject: geography
text: The movement of Earth around the sun on its elliptical orbit is called
explanation: The movement of Earth around the sun on its elliptical orbit is known as revolution ( it takes approximately 36525 days to complete ). Rotation, on the other hand, refers to the Earth's spin on its axis, which takes approximately 24 hours to complete

subject: geography
text: The rock that has the capacity to retain underground water is
explanation: Permeable rock can retain underground water. They can also be referred to as an aquifer, which is a body of rock and/or sediment that holds groundwater

subject: geography
text: Which of the following is a deposition feature of an arid region?
explanation: The depositional features of Arid regions are ripple marks, sand dunes, longitudinal dunes, transverse dunes, Barchans, parabolic dunes, star dunes and loess.The most common depositional feature of arid regions is a barchan, whose dunes are crescent-shaped.

subject: geography
text: Which of the drainage patterns is best established in the areas of alternate hard and soft rocks?
explanation: Trellis drainage patterns are formed where hard and soft formations exist on both banks of the main river, and are reflective of height, accentuated by erosion.

subject: geography
text: The time at the Greenwich meridian is 10:00 a.m. What would the time be at 30ºc
explanation: The Earth rotates one in 24hours So, 360° = 24hrs 1° = 4mins To rotate 30°E The time = 30 x 4mins = 120 mins = 2hrs

subject: geography
text: On March 21 each year, all towns in the Southern Hemisphere experience what?

options.D: equal lengths of day and night

explanation: The March equinox, also called the vernal equinox, marks the beginning of spring in the Northern Hemisphere and autumn in the Southern Hemisphere. All towns in the Southern Hemisphere experience equal length of day and night, i.e day and 12hrs night

subject: geography
text: Which of the following wave actions is a process of erosion?
explanation: The four main processes by which waves erode are - Hydraulic action - compression -Abrasion - Attrition
